title.en: The Real Reason Behind the Korean "Bill Battle"
title.ko: 오늘은 진짜 제가 살게요! (식당에서 계산할 때)

description.en: It's not just about "Jeong." Learn the logic of reciprocity and how to gracefully handle the bill when you want to pay back a friend's kindness.
description.ko: 한국인들이 계산대에서 서로 내겠다고 하는 진짜 이유! 저번에 얻어먹은 고마움을 갚으려는 마음과 경제적 상황에 따른 배려의 리액션을 배워봅니다.

tags: Situation. Food. Restaurant. Payment. Culture. Reciprocity. Season 2

content.speaker: John
content.text: Mina, I used to think Koreans fought at the cash register just to be polite.

content.speaker: John
content.text: But today I saw two friends, and it seemed like they were keeping score of who paid last time. Is there a deeper reason?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 역시 예리하네요! 단순히 예의를 차리는 게 아니에요.
content.translation: As expected, John, you're sharp! It's not just about being polite.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 보통 경제적 여유가 있는 사람이 먼저 내고 다음에 그 고마움을 갚으려는 마음이 섞여 있는 거죠.
content.translation: Usually, the person with more financial room pays first, and the other person wants to repay that kindness later.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Ah, so if you treated me last time, I feel like it's my turn today to relieve your burden?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요! 물론 요즘은 더치페이도 많이 하지만 여전히 친구 사이엔 이런 행동이 많아요.
content.translation: Right! Of course, 'Dutch pay' is common these days, but there are still many reactions like this among friends.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 오늘은 보답의 리액션을 제대로 배워볼게요!
content.translation: Today, we'll properly learn those 'pleasant repayment' reactions!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 저번에 사주셨잖아요. This is like saying "You treated me last time," right?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 네! "이번엔 내 차례다" 라는 명분을 만드는 아주 좋은 말이에요.
content.translation: Yes! It's a very good way to create a justification like "It's my turn this time."

insight.title: 저번에 사주셨잖아요
insight.usage_tip: '저번(Last time)' + '에(At)' + '사주셨잖아요(You bought/treated, right?)'. Use this to remind the other person that it's now your turn to pay.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 아니에요. 저번에 사주셨잖아요.
content.translation: No, Mina. You treated me last time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: What if I want to tell them not to worry about the money?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그럴 때는 부담 갖지 마세요 라고 해보세요.
content.translation: In that case, try saying "부담 갖지 마세요".

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "내가 사고 싶어서 사는 거니까 편하게 생각해라" 라는 배려의 표현이죠.
content.translation: It's an expression of consideration, meaning "I'm buying because I want to, so think of it comfortably."

insight.title: 부담 갖지 마세요
insight.usage_tip: '부담(Burden)' + '갖지 마세요(Don't have/take)'. A kind way to reassure someone who feels guilty about being treated.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Okay, let's jump in. Mina treated me to a big dinner last week when I was broke. Today, I got my paycheck!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 오늘도 제가 낼게요. 지난번에 맛있게 드셔서 보기 좋더라고요.
content.translation: John, I'll pay today too. It was good to see you enjoying the meal last time.

content.speaker: John
content.emotion: 미소 지으며 미나의 카드를 정중히 막는다
content.text: 아뇨, 미나 씨. 오늘은 진짜 제가 살게요.
content.translation: No, Mina. Today, I'm really buying.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 에이! 존은 아직 학생이잖아요. 제가 낼게요!
content.translation: Eh, John, you're still a student, so it's okay. I'll pay!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 아니에요. 저번에 미나씨가 사주셨잖아요. 저 오늘 월급 받았거든요!
content.translation: No. You treated me last time. I got my paycheck today!

insight.title: 저번에 사주셨잖아요
insight.usage_tip: Using your recent good news (getting paid) makes it easier for the other person to accept your treat.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 진짜요? 축하해요! 그럼 부담 안 갖고 잘 얻어먹을게요.
content.translation: Ah, really? Congratulations! Then I'll enjoy the meal without feeling burdened.

insight.title: 잘 얻어먹을게요
insight.usage_tip: A polite and friendly way to accept someone's offer to pay after a bit of back-and-forth.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 네! 다음에도 제가 맛있는 거 사드릴게요!
content.translation: Yes! I'll buy you something delicious next time too!

content.speaker: John
content.text: I love this! It's not just about the money, it's about taking turns and looking out for each other.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 상황에 따라 서로를 배려하는 리액션이 한국식 우정의 핵심이거든요.
content.translation: Right. Reacting with consideration for each other depending on the situation is the core of Korean friendship.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review our "bill battle" reactions one last time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 오늘은 제가 살게요!
content.translation: Today, I'll buy!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 저번에 사주셨잖아요!
content.translation: You treated me last time!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 부담 갖지 마세요!
content.translation: Please don't feel burdened!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 이번엔 제가 대접할게요!
content.translation: This time, I'll treat you!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 잘 먹었습니다!
content.translation: I ate well! / Thank you for the meal!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분도 고마운 사람에게 마음을 전하고 싶을 때 당당하고 기분 좋게 말해 보세요. "오늘은 제가 살게요!"
content.translation: Everyone, when you want to convey your heart to someone you're thankful for, speak up confidently and pleasantly. "Today, I'll buy!"

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's all for today. See you in the next episode.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 또 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: See you again next time. Bye~!

vocabulary.example: 도움을 주신 분들께 작은 선물로 보답했어요. (I reciprocated those who helped me with a small gift.)
거절할 때는 확실한 명분이 필요해요. (You need a clear justification when you refuse.)
과한 선물은 오히려 상대방에게 부담을 줄 수 있어요. (An excessive gift can actually give a burden to the other person.)
서로를 배려하는 마음이 아름다운 우정을 만들어요. (A heart of mutual consideration creates a beautiful friendship.)
첫 월급을 받아서 부모님께 선물을 드렸어요. (I received my first paycheck and gave a gift to my parents.)
친구 덕분에 맛있는 저녁을 잘 얻어먹었어요. (Thanks to my friend, I was treated to a delicious dinner.)
